prompt: Vertical portrait 9:16 high-resolution atlas world map for a Deadlands-inspired Weird West RPG setting. Terrain type: dual_continent. Draw two large fictional continents in aged sepia parchment frontier cartography, separated by a dramatic non-vertical ocean passage: diagonal and S-shaped straits with gulfs, narrows, shoals, broken coast shelves, and twisting channels rather than a simple straight split. The northern/eastern landmass has snowy mountains, pine frontier, ghost-rock rail towns, haunted mining basins, and storm cliffs; the southern/western landmass has desert badlands, red mesas, dry canyons, bayou deltas, sacred spirit lands, and undead outlaw territories. Complex realistic coastlines on both continents: peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, broken shores, curved coasts, river deltas, fjords, capes, lagoons. Islands are unevenly scattered between and around the continents with varied size and shape, including a flooded Great Maze archipelago feel. Add frontier rails, mine markers, occult survey symbols, ghost-rock steam-tech mood, no readable labels, no legible text, no logos, no modern satellite style.
